page.content: In the 2Q FY2025 report for Accenture's Employee Attrition Rates and Utilization Rates by Practice, the overall attrition rate is 21.0%, rising by 2.4% from Q1 FY2025. By department, the IT attrition rate stands at 19.5%, HR at 23.0%, Finance at 20.5%, Sales at 22.0%, and Marketing at 18.0%, culminating in a total departmental attrition of 20.0%. Regionally, Europe shows a total attrition rate of 20.5%. High attrition is noted at 23.5%, medium at 19.0%, and low at 16.0%. The overall summary indicates an attrition rate of 20.0%, with goals set to reduce by 2% and increase retention.

page.content: Employee satisfaction has improved to 78.0%, with a target rate of 18.0% and future projections between 15-18%.

lines.text: Employee satisfaction has improved to 78.0%, with a target rate of 18.0% and future projections between 15-18%.

page.content: The utilization rates by practice area reveal Consulting at 85.5%, Technology at 78.0%, and Outsourcing at 90.3%. Revenue from Consulting is CHF30M with a year-on-year change of 10.2%, Technology revenue is CHF25M with a 5.0% increase, while Outsourcing's revenue is not specified. This comprehensive overview delineates the current state of employee attrition and utilization across various departments and practices, essential for strategic planning and operational efficiency.
